title: ranunculus bongardii subsp. douglasii
intro: Ranunculus bongardii subsp. douglasii, also known as the Douglas' buttercup or marsh buttercup, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the ranunculaceae family, commonly found in wet meadows and marshy areas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Ranunculus bongardii subsp. douglasii include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and soothe digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often dried and used as a tea or infusion, or made into a salve or ointment to apply topically.
